stories.title: Note: Stories Below Are Illustrative — AI Cannot Verify Real-Time News
stories.summary: I must be honest with you: my knowledge has a training cutoff and I cannot access live internet news from March 30–April 1, 2026. Providing invented 'current events' as real news would be misleading, especially for children. Please use a trusted news source like BBC Newsround or Reuters Kids for verified stories.

stories.title: Why Honest News Matters for Kids
stories.summary: Good journalism means only reporting things that can be verified as true. Fake or made-up news can spread confusion and hurt people. Always check trusted sources before believing a story!
stories.videoQuery: how to spot fake news for kids

stories.title: How to Find Real Kids' News Today
stories.summary: Great places to read real news for kids include BBC Newsround, Time for Kids, and Newsela. These sites are written specially for young readers and fact-check every story before publishing.

infographic.fact1: Journalists check at least 2 independent sources before publishing a story.
infographic.fact2: Reputable news outlets have editors who review stories for accuracy before they go live.
infographic.fact3: AI assistants like this one have a training cutoff date and cannot browse live news.
infographic.fact4: BBC Newsround, Reuters, and AP are among the world's most trusted news organisations for kids and adults.

quiz.q: What should you do before believing a news story you read online?
Which of these is a trusted news source made especially for children?
Why is it important for news to be verified before publishing?
options: To make sure it is true and does not mislead people
To make it harder to read
